studies.title: Health Economic Evaluation of  costs and benefits of a computer based program called "Deprexis" used for insured persons of the DAK (statutory health insurance) suffering from depression

_verification.reason: The study is specifically about the digital therapeutic product 'deprexis', as evidenced by the repeated mention of 'Deprexis' in the title, full text, and trial registration. The study evaluates the health economic impact and effectiveness of 'deprexis' for treating depression among insured persons of the DAK.

_classification.reason: The study involves random allocation of participants to an intervention group using the 'Deprexis' program and a control group without access to the program, which is characteristic of a randomized controlled trial.

studies.title: Efficacy of a depression-focused internet intervention in slot machine gamblers: A randomized controlled trial

_verification.reason: The study specifically investigates the efficacy of the 'Deprexis' online intervention for depression in a sample of slot machine gamblers. The product name 'Deprexis' is explicitly mentioned multiple times in the trial description and intervention details.

_classification.reason: The study randomly assigns participants to an intervention group or a wait-list control group, which is a defining feature of a randomized controlled trial (RCT).

studies.title: Digital Health Applications during the waiting period for outpatient psychotherapy: a randomized controlled trial on effectiveness, acceptance and use

_verification.reason: The study specifically investigates the use of the digital therapeutic product deprexis® during the waiting period for outpatient psychotherapy. The product name deprexis® is mentioned multiple times in the full text, confirming the study's focus on this specific intervention.
